problems.titleEn: Question 1 - Dice comparison probability
problems.titleZh: 问题 1 - 两个骰子的大小比较概率
problems.promptEn: Roll two dice. What is the probability that one is larger than the other?
problems.promptZh: 掷两个骰子。一个骰子的点数大于另一个骰子的概率是多少？
problems.explanation: The easiest thing to do here is to draw the matrix of possibilities. (You don't actually have to fill in all the values like in the illustration; drawing a 6 \times 6 block should be enough to figure out the answer.) Now, depending on what the interviewer meant by the question, the answer is one of the following: 1. Asking for the probability of one die being larger than the other (and not caring which one) is analogous to asking for the probability that the two dice are not the same. That is everything but the diagonal, 36 −6 = 30 36 = 5 6. 2. If the interviewer wanted the probability of die A being more than die B, then the possibilities are everything in the upper/lower triangle of the matrix (half of the answer given above), 36 = 5 12. When you're given a question like this, your immediate reaction might be to start writing out the calculations—tedious sums or integrals. Resist this urge. This family of questions (quick probability calculations) can be easily solved by drawing a square on which you can calculate the area without doing integrals. Other examples are the Stick Breaking problem (question 21) and the Romeo and Juliet problem (question 24). This simple example shows the importance of interview preparation. You might think it a given that a rigorous solution is preferred to a back-of-the-envelope one, but you need to keep in mind that interviews aren't testing for job preparedness. They are probably trying to test for several qualities, but they end up selecting the candidates who are the most prepared for brainteasers. The format is akin to asking candidates to do a Rubik's cube and preferring the one who memorised the solution by rote and solves it in two minutes, rather than the one who takes the cube home and solves it over the course of three months without looking at the solution online. Who would you rather have work for you? Understand that brainteasers favour the former candidate and keep it in mind during preparation. Use the pretty solution during the interview and leave the rigorous integrals for a Saturday night with a glass of whiskey in your study, while reflecting on the fact that people in quantitative finance are probably all brainteaser savants.

problems.titleEn: Question 2 - Expected maximum of correlated normals
problems.titleZh: 问题 2 - 相关正态变量最大值的期望
problems.promptEn: Let (X, Y)^T follow a bivariate normal distribution with mean vector (0, 0)^T and covariance matrix [[1, ρ], [ρ, 1]]. Find E[max(X, Y)].
problems.promptZh: 设 (X, Y)^T 服从均值向量为 (0, 0)^T、协方差矩阵为 [[1, ρ], [ρ, 1]] 的二元正态分布。求 E[max(X, Y)]。
problems.explanation: Let Z = max(X, Y). By symmetry, P(X > Y) = 1/2, and the two conditional contributions are equal, so E[Z] = E[X | X > Y]. Set W1 = X and W2 = X - Y. Then (W1, W2) is jointly normal, Var(W2) = 2(1 - ρ), and E[W1 | W2 = w] = w/2. Therefore E[X | X > Y] = E[W1 | W2 > 0] = (1/2)E[W2 | W2 > 0]. For a zero-mean normal variable with variance 2(1 - ρ), the positive-truncation mean is 2 sqrt(1 - ρ) / sqrt(π). Hence E[max(X, Y)] = sqrt((1 - ρ) / π). The attached source image shows the contour and surface plots used in the original explanation.

problems.titleEn: Question 3 - Build an R matrix from vectors
problems.titleZh: 问题 3 - 用多个向量构造 R 矩阵
problems.promptEn: How would you make a matrix from a bunch of vectors in R?
problems.promptZh: 在 R 中，如何用一组向量构造矩阵？
problems.explanation: Assuming the vectors are all of the same size, you can use cbind() to bind the vectors as columns or rbind() to bind the vectors as rows. If you get this question, you probably mentioned R (R Core Team, 2017) on your CV, and the interviewer is testing to see whether you actually use it, or it was just CV fluff. Be prepared for similar questions about all the languages you mention on your CV.

problems.titleEn: Question 4 - Python negative list index
problems.titleZh: 问题 4 - Python 列表负索引
problems.promptEn: If mylist is a list in Python, what is mylist[-1]?
problems.promptZh: 如果 mylist 是 Python 中的列表，mylist[-1] 表示什么？
problems.explanation: It returns the last item in mylist. This question is asked in the same spirit as the previous one. They are probably checking whether you will confuse it with R, where myvec[-1] will return everything except the first entry of the vector myvec.
problems.explanationEn: It returns the last item in mylist. This question is asked in the same spirit as the previous one. They are probably checking whether you will confuse it with R, where myvec[-1] will return everything except the first entry of the vector myvec.
problems.explanationZh: 它返回 mylist 中的最后一项。这个问题的提出与上一个问题的精神相同。他们可能正在检查您是否会将其与 R 混淆，其中 myvec[-1] 将返回除向量 myvec 的第一个条目之外的所有内容。

problems.titleEn: Question 5 - C++ virtual functions
problems.titleZh: 问题 5 - C++ 虚函数
problems.promptEn: What is a C++ virtual function, and why do we need them?
problems.promptZh: 什么是 C++ 虚函数？为什么需要虚函数？
problems.explanation: This is question 7.10 from Joshi et al. (2008) and their wording is probably better than mine. Virtual functions allow for the deferment of the implementation of a function. When you define a virtual function in a base class, you don't have to specify its workings—you can do that later in the classes that inherit from it. For instance, you might have a base class called ContinuousDistribution with a virtual function called simulateRandomVariable. This way, you don't have to implement the function that does the simulation in the base class. Instead, you can define it for each of the implemented distributions. You can also implement a general, inefficient function in the base class and override it in the derived classes with more appropriate functions for each distribution. The example I give is different from Joshi et al. (2008, question 7.10), and I suggest that you think of an example which is germane to your background. It is much easier to remember your own example in an interview than it is to remember someone else's. It also conveys actual working knowledge rather than prepared responses—whether merited or not. The second part of the question is harder to answer and interviewers are likely to have different views. I was asked why we need virtual functions if we can probably use function pointers and other C++ tricks to mimic their behaviour. In truth, virtual functions aren't only used to defer implementation. The academic answer is “It is used to give run time polymorphism,” but make sure you know what this means if you want to use it. It is best explained through an example, like the one given in appendix B.2. Use a base-class pointer to point to a base class (Car, in the example), as well as a derived class (ElectricCar, in the example). When you call a function using the base-class pointer, the compiler decides at run time which function to call, and in the case of a virtual function it will prefer the derived-class version. This ability is hard to mimic without virtual functions—you would have to overload a lot of functions outside of the classes themselves. With virtual functions, the programmer only has to worry about specifying all the correct functions in their class, rather than also dealing with an additional list of external functions to overload. If you mentioned C++ on your CV you should be able to answer all the questions in Joshi et al. (2008, chap. 7). Study it even if you think you know the language well; it has all the important questions along with the answers as the interviewers want to hear them. I suggest familiarising yourself with Joshi (2008) for a tutorial on some C++ design patterns and how they apply to derivative pricing. At the very least, be ready to answer questions about virtual functions, polymorphism, and some design patterns in C++. Questions about differences between Python and C++ are also popular.

problems.explanationZh: 这是 Joshi 等人提出的问题 7.10。 （2008）他们的措辞可能比我的好。虚函数允许推迟函数的实现。当您在基类中定义虚函数时，您不必指定其工作原理 - 您可以稍后在从它继承的类中指定它的工作方式。例如，您可能有一个名为 ContinuousDistribution 的基类，其中包含一个名为 SimulateRandomVariable 的虚函数。这样，您就不必在基类中实现进行模拟的函数。相反，您可以为每个已实现的发行版定义它。您还可以在基类中实现通用的、低效的函数，并在派生类中使用适合每个发行版的更合适的函数来重写它。我给出的例子与 Joshi 等人的例子不同。 （2008，问题7.10），我建议你想一个与你的背景密切相关的例子。在面试中记住自己的例子比记住别人的例子容易得多。它还传达了实际的工作知识，而不是准备好的答案——无论是否值得。问题的第二部分更难回答，面试官可能有不同的观点。有人问我，如果我们可以使用函数指针和其他 C++ 技巧来模仿虚拟函数的行为，为什么我们需要虚拟函数。事实上，虚函数不仅仅用于推迟实现。学术答案是“它用于提供运行时多态性”，但如果您想使用它，请确保您知道这意味着什么。最好通过一个例子来解释，如附录 B.2 中给出的例子。使用基类指针指向基类（示例中为 Car）以及派生类（示例中为 ElectricCar）。当您使用基类指针调用函数时，编译器在运行时决定调用哪个函数，对于虚函数，它会更喜欢派生类版本。如果没有虚函数，这种能力就很难模仿——你必须重载类本身之外的许多函数。使用虚函数，程序员只需担心在其类中指定所有正确的函数，而不必处理要重载的额外外部函数列表。如果您在简历中提到 C++，您应该能够回答 Joshi 等人中的所有问题。 （2008 年，第 7 章）。即使您认为自己很了解这门语言，也要学习它；它拥有所有 重要的问题以及面试官希望听到的答案。我建议您熟悉 Joshi (2008)，了解有关一些 C++ 设计模式以及它们如何应用于衍生品定价的教程。至少，准备好回答有关 C++ 中的虚函数、多态性和一些设计模式的问题。关于 Python 和 C++ 之间差异的问题也很受欢迎。

problems.titleEn: Question 6 - Fourth moment of a normal random variable
problems.titleZh: 问题 6 - 正态随机变量的四阶矩
problems.promptEn: Derive E[X^4] where X follows Normal(0, σ^2).
problems.promptZh: 设 X 服从 Normal(0, σ^2)，推导 E[X^4]。
problems.explanation: Begin by integrating. Derive the fourth moment, E(X4) = Z ∞ −∞ x4f(x)dx = Z ∞ −∞ x4 √ 2πσ exp −x2 2σ2 dx. (3) There doesn't appear to be a quick way to do this integral, so use integration by parts: Z ∞ −∞ udv = uv x=∞ x=−∞− Z ∞ −∞ vdu. The best way to rewrite (3) in order to choose u and v is by trial and error. Be sure to tell the interviewer what you are doing while you are writing out the formula for integration by parts, and mention that you are trying to find the best candidates for u and v. The only candidate that gives you something easier to deal with after integration by parts is E(X4) = √ 2πσ Z ∞ −∞ x3 x exp −x2 2σ2 dx. Then you have u = x3 dv = x exp −x2 2σ2 dx du = 3x2dx v = −σ2 exp −x2 2σ2 giving √ 2πσ ∞ Z −∞ x3 x exp −x2 2σ2 = − √ 2πσ x3σ2 exp −x2 2σ2 x=∞ x=−∞ + √ 2πσ ∞ Z −∞ σ2 exp −x2 2σ2 3x2dx. (4) The first term on the right hand side is equal to zero. Use L'Hˆopital's rule to prove it.5 Apply 5But be wary: I once got reprimanded during an interview at a hipster hedge fund for suggesting L'Hˆopital's rule as a solution to a problem that had the form ∞/∞. The interviewer had never heard of L'Hˆopital, and snidely enquired whether it was some sort of food. I was impressed that someone managed to graduate with a physics degree without ever encountering L'Hˆopital. The interviewer must have been great at brainteasers to get hired. the rule three times: lim x→∞−x3σ2 exp −1 2σ2 x2 = lim x→∞ −x3σ2 exp 1 2σ2 x2 rewrite as fraction = lim x→∞ −3σ2x2 x σ2 exp x2 2σ2 L'Hˆopital 1 = lim x→∞ −6σ2x σ2 1 + x2 σ2 exp x2 2σ2 L'Hˆopital 2 = lim x→∞ −6σ2 x σ4 3 + x2 σ2 exp x2 2σ2 L'Hˆopital 3 = 0 (5) and the same is true for x →−∞. While I include the elaborate calculations above, you don't have to, because it is clear that the denominator will always include a term involving exp x2 2σ2 while the nominator will eventually become zero after enough differentiation. So now (4) becomes √ 2πσ ∞ Z −∞ x4 exp −x2 2σ2 dx = 3σ2 √ 2πσ ∞ Z −∞ x2 exp −x2 2σ2 dx. The right hand side is equal to 3σ2 multiplied by the second moment of a normal distribution with mean zero and standard deviation σ. Recall that if X ∼Normal(µ, σ2) you have Var(X) = E(X2) −E(X)2 σ2 = E(X2) −µ2 meaning E(X2) = σ2 when µ = 0. Thus the fourth moment of X ∼Normal 0, σ2 is E(X4) = 3σ2 E(X2) = 3σ4. This was a very long journey to arrive at the answer, and doing the integration may not have been the best approach. This approach does, however, allow you to exhibit your integration prowess, which may hurt or help you depending on how the interviewer feels. In my case, I wasn't able to arrive at the right answer, but when I reached (4) the interviewer said “there, now we have something familiar” (referring to the second moment) and went on to the next question. A better approach might have been to use the Moment Generating Function of the normal distribution, M(t) = eµte 2 σ2t2. Then you can calculate any moment using E (Xn) = M (n) X (0) = dnMX(t) dtn t=0 where M (n) X (t) is the nth derivative of the function M(t). In the current case where µ = 0, MX(t) = e 2 σ2t2 M (1) X (t) = σ2te σ2t2 M (2) X (t) = σ2 σ2t2 + 1 e σ2t2 M (3) X (t) = σ4t σ2t2 + 3 e σ2t2 M (4) X (t) = σ4 σ4t4 + 6σ2t2 + 3 e σ2t2 M (4) X (0) = 3σ4. Still tedious, but perhaps less so than the integration.6 Of course, you could just learn the moments of the normal distribution by heart, but I don't think that's what interviewers want— they want to see some mathematics. In my case, the interviewer also asked me what the fourth moment meant. I didn't understand, but they probably wanted me to mention the kurtosis, which is a measure of the “tailedness” of a distribution. The fourth moment is used to measure kurtosis, the third moment is related to the skewness, the second moment to the variance, and the first moment is the mean. Robert Tillman suggested the following clever solution, which is the shortest method to derive E X4 for the given normal distribution. Recognise Var(X2) = E (X2)2 −E X2 2 = E X4 −E X2 2 E X4 = Var(X2) + E X2 2 and that X2 σ2 ∼χ2 1. The χ2 k distribution has a mean of k and variance of 2k. Define a random variable Z ∼χ2 1 and write X2 = σ2Z E X2 = σ2 E(Z) = σ2(1) Var X2 = (σ2)2 Var(Z) = σ4(2) giving the same answer as above, E X4 = 2σ4 + σ4 = 3σ4. In my experience, interviewers from investment banks love questions about the normal distribution. This might be in response to interview solution manuals like Crack (2004) and Joshi 6I used Sympy by Meurer et al. (2017) to generate these, and for all the differentiation in (5). et al. (2008), and the fact that they don't contain many questions on the theory of the normal distribution. Perhaps there is a book called “Interesting Proofs about the Normal Distribution” doing the rounds at the banks, or maybe there are internal study groups who meet regularly to make and solve brainteasers about the normal distribution. Whatever it is, I cannot guarantee that these questions will remain popular. I nonetheless recommend that you know the normal distribution well—not only because it might help you during interviews, but also because it is, by now, an overused distribution that you are sure to encounter in practice.

Use L'Hˆopital's rule to prove it.5 Apply 5But be wary: I once got reprimanded during an interview at a hipster hedge fund for suggesting L'Hˆopital's rule as a solution to a problem that had the form ∞/∞. The interviewer had never heard of L'Hˆopital, and snidely enquired whether it was some sort of food. I was impressed that someone managed to graduate with a physics degree without ever encountering L'Hˆopital. While I include the elaborate calculations above, you don't have to, because it is clear that the denominator will always include a term involving exp x2 2σ2 while the nominator will eventually become zero after enough differentiation. So now (4) becomes √ 2πσ ∞ Z −∞ x4 exp −x2 2σ2 dx = 3σ2 √ 2πσ ∞ Z −∞ x2 exp −x2 2σ2 dx. A better approach might have been to use the Moment Generating Function of the normal distribution, M(t) = eµte 2 σ2t2. Define a random variable Z ∼χ2 1 and write X2 = σ2Z E X2 = σ2 E(Z) = σ2(1) Var X2 = (σ2)2 Var(Z) = σ4(2) giving the same answer as above, E X4 = 2σ4 + σ4 = 3σ4. In my experience, interviewers from investment banks love questions about the normal distribution. This might be in response to interview solution manuals like Crack (2004) and Joshi 6I used Sympy by Meurer et al. (2017) to generate these, and for all the differentiation in (5). et al. (2008), and the fact that they don't contain many questions on the theory of the normal distribution. Perhaps there is a book called “Interesting Proofs about the Normal Distribution” doing the rounds at the banks, or maybe there are internal study groups who meet regularly to make and solve brainteasers about the normal distribution. Whatever it is, I cannot guarantee that these questions will remain popular. I nonetheless recommend that you know the normal distribution well—not only because it might help you during interviews, but also because it is, by now, an overused distribution that you are sure to encounter in practice.

problems.titleEn: Question 7 - Missing number in an array
problems.titleZh: 问题 7 - 数组中缺失的数字
problems.promptEn: You have an unsorted array containing integers 1, 2, 3, ..., n, but one number is missing. Describe an algorithm to find the missing number and discuss its complexity.
problems.promptZh: 有一个未排序数组，包含 1, 2, 3, ..., n 中除一个数字外的所有整数。请描述找出缺失数字的算法，并讨论复杂度。
problems.explanation: This is question 5.4 from Joshi et al. (2008). For the case of one missing number, you know the sum is supposed to be n(n + 1), so you can calculate the sum of the n numbers, and the difference will be the missing number. The computational complexity of this is O(n), since you need to add the n −1 numbers. Answer 7.a): (Question on page 11) This is a little harder to figure out. If you struggle, think about the case with two missing numbers. Then think of three. This step-by-step thinking should help you to arrive at the general case. For many brainteasers, it helps to start with the case of n = 1 and then generalise, as discussed in appendix A. The general solution to this question is similar to the case of one missing number. You have n −k numbers x1, x2, x3,..., xn−k. To find the k missing values, calculate n−k X i=1 xi n−k X i=1 x2 i n−k X i=1 x3 i... n−k X i=1 xk i and compare these with their theoretical values. The result is a system of k equations to solve, with k unknowns. The answers represent the missing numbers. For instance, assume you have three missing numbers, m1, m2, and m3, which allows you to set up the following equations: Theoretical value z \}| \{ n(n + 1) = n−3 X i=1 xi + m1 + m2 + m3 n(n + 1)(2n + 1) = n−3 X i=1 x2 i + m2 1 + m2 2 + m2 n2(n + 1)2 = n−3 X i=1 x3 i + m3 1 + m3 2 + m3 3. You have three equations, and three unknowns. You will probably have to solve this numerically, possibly by doing an exhaustive search over the integers from 1 to n. For the theoretical values, use Faulhaber's formula: n X k=1 kp = p + 1 p X j=0 (−1)j p + 1 j Bjnp+1−j where Bj represents the Bernoulli numbers, or calculate them numerically. An alternative way to answer this question was suggested by Jonathan Hon. It involves initialising a boolean array of size n, with all the elements set to false. Then you loop over the array of integers and use each integer to set the value in the corresponding position (or position minus one for zero-indexed arrays) in the boolean array to true. Now you can loop over the boolean array and look for the values that are false, revealing the missing numbers. The difference between the two approaches is the complexity. The first approach has space complexity of O(k) since you only have to store the k equations, but you also have to solve them. The computational complexity of solving them is difficult to calculate, and it will depend on the algorithm you use. Worst case scenario is the brute-force approach, where you try all the possible values, which will have a worst-case complexity of O(n!/((n −k)!k!)), as we have to try all the combinations of k integers. The second approach has a space complexity of O(n), since you have to store the boolean array, and a computational complexity of O(n). There is, however, more nuance to this method. If, instead of using a boolean array, you use an integer array and put the integers in their corresponding places, you will end up sorting the numbers. The best sorting algorithms require O(n log(n)) operations. Have you cheated? Kind of, but that is because you have more information than you would when sorting an arbitrary list of numbers; you know that the numbers are the integers from 1 to n, and you know there are only k missing. This is something that you may have to debate with the interviewer, but usually discussions like these are a sign that the interview is going well. A question may have a best or right answer, but this isn't necessarily the answer the interviewer wants to hear. Often you have to allow the interviewer to guide you to the answer they want. Answer 7.b): (Question on page 11) Finding one missing number in a sorted array is a trivial exercise. Simply check that the array starts at one, then go through it checking that each number is one more than the previous one. Any gaps represent missing numbers. The complexity of this is O(n), as you have to go through each value in the array. This is the same complexity as the problem with one missing number in an unsorted array, which is suspicious; knowing that the array is sorted gives much more information. Of course, you can do better. You can check the number in the middle of the array against the expected one and then you know whether the missing value is in the top or bottom half of the array. Repeat until you find the offending value. This approach is known as binary search and has complexity of O(log2(n)).

problems.explanationZh: 这是 Joshi 等人提出的问题 5.4。 （2008）。对于缺少一个数字的情况，您知道总和应该是 n(n + 1)，因此您可以计算 n 个数字的总和，而差值就是缺少的数字。其计算复杂度为 O(n)，因为您需要将 n −1 个数字相加。答案 7.a)：（第 11 页的问题）这有点难以理解。如果您遇到困难，请考虑一下缺少两个数字的情况。然后想到三个。这种逐步思考应该可以帮助您得出一般情况。对于许多脑筋急转弯来说，从 n = 1 的情况开始然后进行概括会有所帮助，如附录 A 中所述。该问题的一般解决方案类似于缺少一个数字的情况。你有 n −k 个数字 x1, x2, x3,...。。。，xn−k。要找到 k 个缺失值，请计算 n−k X i=1 xi n−k X i=1 x2 i n−k X i=1 x3 i... n−k X i=1 xk i 并将其与理论值进行比较。结果是需要求解 k 个方程组，其中有 k 个未知数。答案代表缺失的数字。例如，假设您有三个缺失的数字 m1、m2 和 m3，这允许您建立以下方程： 理论值 z \}| \{ n(n + 1) = n−3 X i=1 xi + m1 + m2 + m3 n(n + 1)(2n + 1) = n−3 X i=1 x2 i + m2 1 + m2 2 + m2 n2(n + 1)2 = n−3 X i=1 x3 i + m3 1 + m3 2 + m3 3. 你有三个方程和三个未知数。您可能需要通过数字方式解决这个问题，可能需要对从 1 到 n 的整数进行详尽的搜索。对于理论值，请使用 Faulhaber 公式： n X k=1 kp = p + 1 p X j=0 (−1)j p + 1 j Bjnp+1−j 其中 Bj 表示伯努利数，或者进行数值计算。乔纳森·洪（Jonathan Hon）提出了回答这个问题的另一种方法。它涉及初始化一个大小为 n 的布尔数组，并将所有元素设置为 false。然后，循环遍历整数数组，并使用每个整数将布尔数组中相应位置（或零索引数组的位置减一）的值设置为 true。现在，您可以循环布尔数组并查找 false 值，从而显示丢失的数字。两种方法之间的区别在于复杂性。第一种方法的空间复杂度为 O(k)，因为您只需存储 k 个方程，但还必须求解它们。解决它们的计算复杂度很难计算，这取决于您使用的算法。最坏的情况是暴力方法，您尝试所有可能的值，最坏情况的复杂度为 O(n!/((n −k)!k!))，因为我们必须尝试 k 个整数的所有组合。第二种方法的空间复杂度为 O(n)，因为您必须存储布尔数组，计算复杂度为 O(n)。然而，这种方法还有更多细微差别。如果不使用布尔数组，而是使用整数数组并将整数放在相应的位置，则最终将对数字进行排序。最好的排序算法需要 O(n log(n)) 次操作。你被骗了吗？有点像，但那是因为你比对任意数字列表进行排序时拥有更多的信息；你知道这些数字是从 1 到 n 的整数，并且你知道只缺少 k。这是你可能需要与面试官争论的事情，但通常这样的讨论是面试进展顺利的标志。一个问题可能有一个最佳或正确的答案，但这不一定是面试官想听到的答案。通常你必须让面试官引导你得到他们想要的答案。答案 7.b)：（第 11 页上的问题）在排序数组中查找一个缺失的数字是一项微不足道的练习。只需检查数组是否从 1 开始，然后检查每个数字是否比前一个数字大 1。任何间隙都代表缺失的数字。其复杂度为 O(n)，因为您必须遍历数组中的每个值。这与未排序数组中缺少一个数字的问题具有相同的复杂性，这是值得怀疑的；知道数组已排序可以提供更多信息。当然，你可以做得更好。您可以将数组中间的数字与预期的数字进行比较，然后就可以知道缺失的值是在数组的上半部分还是下半部分。重复直到找到有问题的值。这种方法称为二分搜索，复杂度为 O(log2(n))。

problems.titleEn: Question 8 - Sampling distributions of normal estimators
problems.titleZh: 问题 8 - 正态分布估计量的抽样分布
problems.promptEn: Suppose Y follows Normal(μ, σ^2). Now, 10^6 people each draw 1000 samples from this distribution. Let i denote the ith person. Each person estimates μ_i and σ_i^2 using their samples Y_1, Y_2, ..., Y_1000. How should they do this? If you draw histograms of the 10^6 estimates of μ_i and σ_i^2, what would their distributions be? How would you prove the exact sampling distribution of σ_i^2?
problems.promptZh: 设 Y 服从 Normal(μ, σ^2)。现在有 10^6 个人，每人从该分布抽取 1000 个样本。令 i 表示第 i 个人。每个人用样本 Y_1, Y_2, ..., Y_1000 来估计 μ_i 和 σ_i^2，应该如何估计？如果画出这 10^6 个 μ_i 和 σ_i^2 估计值的直方图，它们分别会是什么分布？如何证明 σ_i^2 的精确抽样分布？
problems.explanation: This is a question about sampling theory in the frequentist sense. If you have an estimator for the parameter, and you draw repeated samples from the population, what will the distribution of the estimator be? Note that the question isn't Bayesian, since you aren't assuming random parameters, but merely that the estimator will have a distribution due to the random sampling of the values it depends on. The right way to estimate the parameters using the samples are through the sample mean and variance, ˜µi = ¯yi = 1 n n X j=1 yij ˜σ2 i = n −1 n X j=1 (yij −¯yi)2. If you draw histograms of these values, the distribution of ˜µi will be normal, because it is the sum of normal random variables yij and thus also normal. The distribution of ˜σ2 i will be chi-squared, because it is the sum of a bunch of squared normal random variables. The way to prove this is to use the formula for random variable transformation. If Z = g(X) then fZ(x) = fX(g−1(x)) d dxg−1(x) where fZ(x) is the pdf of Z and fX(x) is the pdf of X. When I wrote this down, my interviewer checked his notes and said “No, that is wrong, you have to use the following formula” and produced fZ(z) = fX(g−1(z)) d dz g−1(z). Then he asked me to derive it to make sure I understood where it came from. Here is the derivation: FZ(x) = P(Z < x) = P(g(X) < x) = P(X < g−1(x)) = P(X < g−1(x)) = FX(g−1(x)) fZ(x) = fX(g−1(x)) d dxg−1(x). Notice that I used x in my derivation, not z. The interviewer nodded and moved on to the next question.

problems.titleEn: Question 9 - Derivative of an inverse function
problems.titleZh: 问题 9 - 反函数的导数
problems.promptEn: If we know g'(x), what can we say about d/dx g^{-1}(x)?
problems.promptZh: 如果已知 g'(x)，关于 d/dx g^{-1}(x) 可以说什么？
problems.explanation: This is a question about the relationship between the derivative and the inverse of a function. The answer is simple, if you start correctly. I didn't, so I struggled with it, and after using x instead of z in the previous derivation probably didn't leave a good impression. I produce the correct answer here, x = x g(g−1(x)) = x d dxg(g−1(x)) = d dxx g′(g−1(x)) d dxg−1(x) = 1 (chain rule) d dxg−1(x) = g′(g−1(x)). The crux is knowing to start with the first two lines, and to differentiate on both sides. If you try solving this with integrals, as I did, you are going to have a bad time.

problems.titleEn: Question 10 - Coin-flip gamble and normal approximation
problems.titleZh: 问题 10 - 抛硬币赌博与正态近似
problems.promptEn: You are presented with the following gamble: you flip 100 fair coins. If 60 or more land on heads, you win £10; you win nothing on all other outcomes. Should you play this game for £1?
problems.promptZh: 你面前有这样一个赌博：抛 100 枚公平硬币。如果至少 60 枚为正面，你赢得 10 英镑；其他结果都没有收益。你是否应该花 1 英镑参加这个游戏？
problems.explanation: This can be solved by using the Central Limit Theorem, or the normal approximation to the binomial distribution. You have Y ∼Binomial(p = 0.5, n = 100) and for large n you can approximate the binomial distribution through the normal distribution, Y ∼ approx Normal(np, np(1 −p)). The values of n and p in the problem give np = 100 (1/2) = 50 np(1 −p) = 100 (1/2)2 = 25, resulting in Y ∼Normal 50, (5)2. In order to evaluate whether the gamble is worthwhile you have to calculate its expected return: E(Gamble) = −1 + (10)P(Win) + (0)P(Lose) = −1 + (10)P(Y > 60). Given that you are in an interview, you need to evaluate P(Y > 60) without a calculator. With the normal distribution, approximately 95% of the data lies within two standard deviations of the mean. For the current problem, two standard deviations from the mean is conveniently located at 60, as the following graph indicates: 95% 2.5% The probability of interest is P(Y > 60 = 0.025) and thus the expected value of the gamble is E(Gamble) = −1 + (10)(0.025) = −1 + 0.25 = −0.75. On average you lose 75 pence for playing the game so it is not worthwhile. The fair price, denoted £fair, occurs where the gamble breaks even: E(Gamble) = 0 £fair −0.25 = 0 £fair = 0.25 and this is the highest price at which a rational and risk-neutral person should start agreeing to play. The assumptions of rationality and risk neutrality are very important. If a billionaire gets immeasurable physical pleasure from engaging in risky gambles—and therefore is not risk neutral—the rational course of action is probably to spend all of their excess money on unfair gambles. Conversely, risk-neutral people might not always act rationally.

problems.titleEn: Question 11 - Zero correlation and independence for normals
problems.titleZh: 问题 11 - 正态变量零相关与独立性
problems.promptEn: You have X following Normal(0, 1) and Y following Normal(0, 1). If the correlation coefficient ρ_XY is 0, are X and Y independent?
problems.promptZh: 设 X 服从 Normal(0, 1)，Y 也服从 Normal(0, 1)。如果相关系数 ρ_XY = 0，X 和 Y 是否独立？
problems.explanation: No. Correlation only measures linear dependence. They could have a non-linear dependence. See the next question for an example. Answer 11.a): (Question on page 20) Let Z have the probability mass function p(z) = ( 0.5 if z = −1 0.5 if z = 1 or equivalently P(Z = −1) = 0.5 P(Z = 1) = 0.5. Let X ∼Normal(0, 1) and Y = ZX. Derive the distribution of Y using its cumulative distribution function: P(Y < x) = P(ZX < x|Z = 1)P(Z = 1) + P(ZX < x|Z = −1)P(Z = −1) = P(X < x)(0.5) + P(−X < x)(0.5) = (0.5)(P(X < x) + P(X ≥−x)) = (0.5)(P(X < x) + P(X < x)) symmetry = P(X < x) meaning Y has the same distribution as X. Now, check whether ρ = 0, ρ = Cov(X, Y ) σXσY = Cov(X, Y ) = Cov(X, ZX) = E(XZX) −E(X) E(ZX) = E(X2Z) −E(X) E(ZX) = E(X2) E(Z) −E(X) E(Z) E(X) independence = 0 since E(Z) = 0. You have successfully constructed a situation where there is a clearly defined dependence between Y and X, but they are both distributed Normal(0, 1) with correlation coefficient ρ = 0.

problems.titleEn: Question 12 - Optimal urn allocation
problems.titleZh: 问题 12 - 最优 urn 分球策略
problems.promptEn: You have two urns, five red balls, and five blue balls. You can distribute the balls into the urns any way you like, but each urn must have at least one ball in it. I will choose one urn at random (p = 0.5) and then draw one ball from it. If the ball is blue, you win. How should you distribute the balls to maximize your probability of winning? Log into this pair-programming website and use Python or C++ to solve the problem while I watch.
problems.promptZh: 你有两个 urn、五个红球和五个蓝球。你可以任意分配这些球，但每个 urn 至少要有一个球。我会以 p = 0.5 随机选择一个 urn，再从中抽出一个球；如果抽到蓝球，你就赢。应如何分配球才能最大化获胜概率？请登录结对编程网站，在我观察时用 Python 或 C++ 解决这个问题。
problems.explanation: Let B1 be the number of blue balls in urn 1, and R1 be the number of red balls in urn 1. Then P(Win) = 1 B1 B1 + R1 + 1 B2 B2 + R2 = 1 B1 B1 + R1 + 1 5 −B1 10 −(B1 + R1). (6) You need to find the values for B1 and R1 that minimise this. The interviewer wanted me to explain how I would solve the question, and then we went into an online pair-programming room, where we found the solution in Python using brute force, which in this case was an exploration of the whole solution space. You can also find the solution through reason. If you wanted to maximise the probability of winning in the one bucket, you could put just one red ball in it, and no blue balls. Then the probability of winning becomes P(Win) = 1 2 (1) + 1 4 = 1 13 = 13, which is near 3/4. In fact, for N balls this strategy has a winning probability of 2 (1) + 1 N 2 −1 N −1! = 1 N −1 + N 2 −1 N −1! = 1 2N −2 + N −2 N −1 = 1 3N −4 N −1, which approaches 3/4 as N approach infinity. The hardest part about this question is coming up with (6). Try to understand it well enough to derive it yourself. When I did it during the interview, I started with a notation that made the problem much harder than it is (by having variables for the total number of balls in each of the urns as well). You have to start somewhere, and my first attempt set me offon the wrong path, but the interviewer was interested in the approach and asked me to continue with it. I felt that the interview went pretty well, but the feedback I received was that the interviewer wanted me to “intuit” the answer, rather than solve it using mathematical reasoning. In the past (especially with the Air Force One question in section 2.1), I was told the opposite: use mathematics to solve the question, not intuition and logical reasoning. Online pair-programming interviews are increasingly prevalent with banks betting on auto- mation and competing for programming talent. These interviews usually take two forms. The first is similar to the above, asking you to solve mathematical problems numerically. For in- stance, an on-site exam once asked me to write code, by hand, to numerically solve the Stick Breaking problem from section 2.2. The second form is that of tech-company interviews. That is, programming questions you can expect to get at Google and Facebook, usually involving algorithms and data structures. It is worthwhile to review some of them. Good websites for practise and examples are https://www.hackerrank.com/ and https://www.codewars.com/. Questions about sorting algorithms (see question 26) used to be a favourite, but interviewers have broadened their horizons in recent years. Look for problems involving string manipulation (finding anagrams or longest substrings) like question 22. Other examples I've encountered are question 25 and the Friend Circle problem: “A friend circle is any group of people connected by mutual acquaintances. Given an adjacency matrix of N people, write code to count the number of friend circles.” These last two examples come from graph theory, which is a well-known source of difficult tech-interview questions.

problems.explanationZh: 设 B1 为瓮 1 中的蓝球数量，R1 为瓮 1 中的红球数量。则 P(Win) = 1 B1 B1 + R1 + 1 B2 B2 + R2 = 1 B1 B1 + R1 + 1 5 −B1 10 −(B1 + R1)。 (6) 您需要找到 B1 和 R1 的值来最小化这种情况。面试官想让我解释一下如何解决这个问题，然后我们进入了一个在线结对编程室，在那里我们使用蛮力在 Python 中找到了解决方案，在本例中这是对整个解决方案空间的探索。也可以通过理性去寻找解决办法。如果你想在一个桶中获胜的概率最大化，你可以只在其中放入一颗红球，而不是蓝球。那么获胜的概率就变成 P(Win) = 1 2 (1) + 1 4 = 1 13 = 13，接近 3/4。事实上，对于 N 个球，该策略的获胜概率为 2 (1) + 1 N 2 -1 N -1! = 1 N -1 + N 2 -1 N -1! = 1 2N −2 + N −2 N −1 = 1 3N −4 N −1，当 N 接近无穷大时，该值接近 3/4。这个问题最难的部分是提出（6）。尝试充分理解它，以便自己导出它。当我在采访中这样做时，我开始使用一种使问题变得更加困难的符号（通过为每个瓮中的球总数设置变量）。你必须从某个地方开始，我的第一次尝试让我走上了错误的道路，但面试官对这种方法很感兴趣，并要求我继续这样做。我觉得面试进行得很顺利，但我收到的反馈是面试官希望我“直觉”答案，而不是用数学推理来解决。过去（尤其是第 2.1 节中的空军一号问题），我被告知相反的情况：使用数学来解决问题，而不是直觉和逻辑推理。随着银行押注自动化并争夺编程人才，在线结对编程面试越来越普遍。这些访谈通常采取两种形式。第一个与上面类似，要求你用数值方法解决数学问题。例如，一次现场考试曾经要求我手工编写代码，以数值方式解决 2.2 节中的断棍问题。第二种形式是科技公司面试。也就是说，你可以在 Google 和 Facebook 上找到编程问题，通常涉及算法和数据结构。其中一些值得回顾。适合练习和示例的网站是 https://www.hackerrank.com/ 和 https://www.codewars.com/。 有关排序算法的问题（参见问题 26）曾经是最受欢迎的问题，但近年来面试官的视野有所拓宽。寻找涉及字符串操作（查找字谜或最长子串）的问题，例如问题 22。我遇到的其他示例是问题 25 和朋友圈问题：“朋友圈是由相互熟人连接的任何一群人。给定一个由 N 个人组成的邻接矩阵，编写代码来计算朋友圈的数量。”最后两个例子来自图论，这是众所周知的技术面试难题的来源。

problems.titleEn: Question 13 - Three judges and correct verdict probability
problems.titleZh: 问题 13 - 三位法官给出正确判决的概率
problems.promptEn: There are three judges in a court with probabilities p, p, and 1/2 of reaching the correct verdict. A verdict is decided only if at least two judges agree. What is the probability that the court reaches the correct verdict?
problems.promptZh: 法庭中有三位法官，作出正确判决的概率分别为 p、p 和 1/2。只有至少两位法官意见一致时才形成判决。法庭最终作出正确判决的概率是多少？
problems.explanation: The court reaches the correct verdict if either two or three judges are correct. The obvious solution of 1 −P(All three wrong) ignores the scenario in which one judge is correct and the other two are wrong. You have to write out all the possible combinations: P(Three correct) = pp(1/2) P(Two correct) = pp(1 −1/2) + p(1 −p)(1/2) + (1 −p)p(1/2) = p2 −(1/2)p2 + p −p2 = p −(1/2)p2 P(Correct verdict reached) = P(Two or three correct) = (1/2)p2 + p −(1/2)p2 = p. Note that you cannot use the binomial coefficients, 3 and 3, as the judges don't all have the same p.

Note that you cannot use the binomial coefficients, 3 and 3, as the judges don't all have the same p.
problems.explanationZh: 如果有两个或三个法官是正确的，法院就会做出正确的判决。 1 −P（三个都错）的明显解忽略了一个判断正确而另外两个判断错误的情况。你必须写出所有可能的组合： P(三个正确) = pp(1/2) P(两个正确) = pp(1 −1/2) + p(1 −p)(1/2) + (1 −p)p(1/2) = p2 −(1/2)p2 + p −p2 = p −(1/2)p2 P(达到正确结论) = P(两个或三个正确) = (1/2)p2 + p −(1/2)p2 = p。请注意，您不能使用二项式系数 3 和 3，因为法官的 p 并不相同。

problems.titleEn: Question 14 - Linear regression assumptions and inference
problems.titleZh: 问题 14 - 线性回归假设与推断
problems.promptEn: Suppose you wanted to model Y using X and decided to use the linear regression Y = Xβ + ε. What assumptions are being made? How would you find β? What tests can be done on β afterwards?
problems.promptZh: 假设你想用 X 来建模 Y，并决定使用线性回归 Y = Xβ + ε。这里作出了哪些假设？如何求 β？之后可以对 β 做哪些检验？
problems.explanation: Questions about regression theory come up time and time again, yet they aren't discussed in any of the classic interview texts. Therefore, this text includes the relevant regression theory for interviews in section 3, as well as a crib sheet to use during phone interviews in appendix C.

Therefore, this text includes the relevant regression theory for interviews in section 3, as well as a crib sheet to use during phone interviews in appendix C.
problems.explanationZh: 关于回归理论的问题一次又一次地出现，但在任何经典的访谈文本中都没有讨论它们。因此，本文在第 3 节中包含了访谈的相关回归理论，并在附录 C 中包含了电话访谈期间使用的小抄表。

problems.titleEn: Question 15 - Disease test and Bayes' law
problems.titleZh: 问题 15 - 疾病检测与贝叶斯定律
problems.promptEn: One percent of people in the world have a given disease, and the test for it is imperfect. The test has an 80% chance of showing positive if you have the disease, but if you do not have the disease, there is a 10% chance of a false positive. What is the probability that you actually have the disease if your test result is positive?
problems.promptZh: 世界上 1% 的人患有某种疾病，而该疾病的检测并不完美。若患病，检测有 80% 的概率呈阳性；若未患病，检测有 10% 的概率假阳性。如果检测结果为阳性，你实际患病的概率是多少？
problems.explanation: Bayes' law questions are extremely common, both in phone and face-to-face interviews. The hardest part is turning the words into their respective conditional probabilities. Start with what you want—the probability that you actually have the disease if your rest results are positive. Put differently, P(Dyes|Test+), where Dyes means you have the disease, Dno means you are disease-free, and Test+ or Test−indicates the result of your test. Using Bayes' law, re-express this as P(Dyes|Test+) = P(Test+|Dyes)P(Dyes) P(Test+|Dyes)P(Dyes) + P(Test+|Dno)P(Dno). Now, consider the information the interviewer gave, and hope that you have all the bits you need. (This doesn't always happen, and it can either indicate that you have some more work to do, or that the interviewer got the question wrong.) “One percent of people in the world have this disease.” P(Dyes) = 0.01 P(Dno) = 0.99. “The test has an 80% chance of showing positive if you have the disease,” P(Test+|Dyes) = 0.8, “... if you do not have the disease, there is a 10% chance of showing a false positive.” P(Test+|Dno) = 0.1. Putting it all together yields P(Dyes|Test+) = (0.8)(0.01) (0.8)(0.01) + (0.1)(0.99). You need an easy way to evaluate this without a calculator, so factorise it as (0.8)(0.01) (0.8)(0.01) + (0.1)(0.99) = (8)(1) (8)(1) + (1)(99) 1000 1000 = and the denominator is a prime number so you can't simplify further. Thus, the answer is a bit less than 8%. (The exact answer is 0.07476636.) You can represent the situation in a picture to get an intuitive understanding. In the unit square below, the shaded area represents everyone who tested positive for the disease. The people in the little square on the bottom left (which is 1% of the square's area) are the ones who actually have the disease. If you tested positive, you fall in the shaded area. The question is asking “given that you fall somewhere in the shaded area, what is the probability that you are also in the little square with people who have the disease.” No Disease Disease As a fraction, that is Small shaded area Small shaded area + Large shaded area = (0.8)(0.01) (0.8)(0.01) + (0.1)(0.99) which is the same as before. The picture reveals the nuances in Bayes' law questions and the reason for their unintuitive answers. There is a low prevalence of the disease, together with a high false-positive rate. Ten percent might not seem high for a false-positive rate, but it results in a large section of the non-diseased population testing positive relative to the few people who actually have the disease. interviews, 3 hours

problems.explanationZh: 贝叶斯定律问题在电话采访和面对面采访中都非常常见。最难的部分是将单词转化为它们各自的条件概率。从你想要的开始——如果你的休息结果呈阳性，你实际上患有这种疾病的可能性。换句话说，P(Dyes|Test+)，其中 Dyes 表示您患有疾病，Dno 表示您没有疾病，Test+ 或 Test− 表示您的测试结果。使用贝叶斯定律，将其重新表示为 P(Dyes|Test+) = P(Test+|Dyes)P(Dyes) P(Test+|Dyes)P(Dyes) + P(Test+|Dno)P(Dno)。现在，考虑一下面试官提供的信息，并希望您掌握了所需的所有信息。 （这种情况并不总是发生，它可能表明你还有更多工作要做，或者面试官问错了问题。）“世界上百分之一的人患有这种疾病。” P（染料）= 0.01 P（Dno）= 0.99。 “如果您患有这种疾病，则测试有 80% 的机会显示出阳性，”P(测试+|染料) = 0.8，“……如果您没有患有这种疾病，则有 10% 的机会显示出假阳性。” P(测试+|Dno) = 0.1。将它们放在一起得出 P(染料|测试+) = (0.8)(0.01) (0.8)(0.01) + (0.1)(0.99)。 您需要一种无需计算器即可计算的简单方法，因此将其分解为 (0.8)(0.01) (0.8)(0.01) + (0.1)(0.99) = (8)(1) (8)(1) + (1)(99) 1000 1000 = 并且分母是质数，因此您无法进一步简化。因此，答案略小于 8%。 （准确答案是0.07476636。）您可以将情况表示在图片中以获得直观的理解。在下面的单位正方形中，阴影区域代表每个疾病检测呈阳性的人。左下角的小方块（占方块面积的 1%）里的人是真正患有这种疾病的人。如果您的检测呈阳性，您就会落在阴影区域。问题是“假设你落在阴影区域的某个地方，那么你也和患有这种疾病的人一起在小方块中的概率是多少。”无疾病 疾病 作为分数，即 小阴影面积 小阴影面积 + 大阴影面积 = (0.8)(0.01) (0.8)(0.01) + (0.1)(0.99) 与之前相同。该图揭示了贝叶斯定律问题的细微差别以及其答案不直观的原因。该病的患病率较低，假阳性率较高。对于假阳性率而言，百分之十似乎并不高，但它导致大部分未患病人群相对于少数实际患有该疾病的人检测呈阳性。 次面试，3小时

problems.titleEn: Question 16 - Derivative of x^x
problems.titleZh: 问题 16 - x^x 的导数
problems.promptEn: What is d/dx x^x?
problems.promptZh: 求 d/dx x^x。
problems.explanation: This is question 6.18 from Joshi et al. (2008). Questions appearing as though they are pure mathematics are usually asking you to do two different things: 1. Simplify and rewrite the problem 2. Solve the problem using integration or differentiation The former is harder, especially if you don't know the trick for that specific question. If you show potential for solving the question, the interviewer will likely give you hints to get to the rewrite trick. The latter part requires calculus from a year-one mathematics course. That is a lot of theory to brush up on, but the rules highlighted in appendix C should be sufficient for most interview questions. The wrong answer is (x −1)xx−1. Here is the rewrite trick that will put you on the right track, xx = eln(xx) = ex ln(x). Follow this by using the chain rule and the product rule. Let ex ln(x) = eu u = x ln(x) du = 1 + ln(x) (Product rule) then d dxex ln(x) = d dueu du dx = eu(1 + ln(x)) = ex ln(x)(1 + ln(x)) = xx(1 + ln(x)).

problems.explanationZh: 这是 Joshi 等人提出的问题 6.18。 （2008）。看似纯数学的问题通常要求您做两件不同的事情： 1. 简化并重写问题 2. 使用积分或微分解决问题 前者更难，特别是如果您不知道该特定问题的技巧。如果你表现出解决问题的潜力，面试官可能会提示你如何重写技巧。后一部分需要一年级数学课程中的微积分。有很多理论需要温习，但附录 C 中强调的规则应该足以解决大多数面试问题。错误答案是 (x −1)xx−1。这里的重写技巧将使您走上正轨，xx = eln(xx) = ex ln(x)。通过使用链式法则和乘积法则来遵循这一点。设 ex ln(x) = eu u = x ln(x) du = 1 + ln(x) （乘积规则），则 d dxex ln(x) = d dueu du dx = eu(1 + ln(x)) = ex ln(x)(1 + ln(x)) = xx(1 + ln(x))。

problems.titleEn: Question 17 - Compare e^π and π^e
problems.titleZh: 问题 17 - 比较 e^π 与 π^e
problems.promptEn: Which is larger, e^π or π^e?
problems.promptZh: e^π 和 π^e 哪个更大？
problems.explanation: This is question 6.6 from Joshi et al. (2008). It is similar to the previous question as it also requires a rewrite trick followed by some calculus. There is no intuitive solution. If you evaluate eπ and πe numerically, one of them is 22.46 and the other one is 23.14. This makes it a beautiful question, but the beauty can quickly evaporate if your first encounter is during an interview. Now for the trick: you have two expressions, eπ πe and you can take the natural logarithm of both without affecting their relative order: log(eπ) log(πe). You can also divide both expressions by πe without influencing their order, since πe is positive, log(eπ) eπ = π log(e) eπ = log(e) e log(πe) eπ = e log(π) eπ = log(π) π. Now you've changed the original question into one about the behaviour of the function log(x) x (7) in the region near e and π. Specifically, you want to know whether (7) is increasing or decreasing over this region. So you take its derivative, using the product rule d dx log x x = log x d dx x + 1 x d dx log x = −log x 1 x2 + 1 x2 = 1 x2 (1 −log x). Now you can evaluate the nature of the function at the two points. At e you have e2 (1 −log e) = 0 and at π you have π2 (1 −log π). Here, the sign will only depend on the sign of (1 −log π). You know that π is slightly larger than e (π ≈3.14 and e ≈2.71) so you can write (1 −log π) = (1 −log e1+k) (8) where k is some small positive quantity. Then (1 −log π) = (1 −(1 + k)) = −k which is negative. Writing it with the k as in (8) actually helps to solve the problem. At e, the derivative is 0, so you know it has a critical point there. For all the values thereafter, k will be increasing so the derivative will be negative, implying the function f(x) = log(x)/x is decreasing after e. Below is a plot of the function with the important points highlighted. e π −0.1 0.1 0.2 0.3 x log(x) x That means log(e) e > log(π) π eπ > πe and a calculator confirms this, eπ = 23.14 πe = 22.46.

problems.explanationZh: 这是 Joshi 等人提出的问题 6.6。 （2008）。它与上一个问题类似，因为它也需要重写技巧，然后进行一些微积分。没有直观的解决方案。如果对 eπ 和 πe 进行数值计算，其中一个是 22.46，另一个是 23.14。这使它成为一个美丽的问题，但如果你第一次遇到是在面试过程中，那么美丽很快就会消失。现在我们来说说技巧：你有两个表达式 eπ πe，你可以在不影响它们相对顺序的情况下取两者的自然对数：log(eπ) log(πe)。您还可以将两个表达式除以 πe 而不会影响它们的顺序，因为 πe 为正数， log(eπ) eπ = π log(e) eπ = log(e) e log(πe) eπ = e log(π) eπ = log(π) π。 现在您已将原来的问题更改为有关函数 log(x) x (7) 在 e 和 π 附近区域中的行为的问题。具体来说，您想知道 (7) 在此区域内是增加还是减少。因此，您可以使用乘积规则 d dx log x x = log x d dx x + 1 x d dx log x = −log x 1 x2 + 1 x2 = 1 x2 (1 −log x) 求其导数。现在您可以在两点评估函数的性质。在 e 处，有 e2 (1 −log e) = 0，在 π 处，有 π2 (1 −log π)。这里，符号仅取决于 (1 −log π) 的符号。您知道 π 略大于 e（π ≈3.14 且 e ≈2.71），因此您可以写成 (1 −log π) = (1 −log e1+k) (8)，其中 k 是某个小的正数。那么 (1 −log π) = (1 −(1 + k)) = −k 为负数。像(8)中那样用k来写实际上有助于解决问题。在 e 处，导数为 0，因此您知道它在那里有一个临界点。对于此后的所有值，k 将增加，因此导数将为负，这意味着函数 f(x) = log(x)/x 在 e 之后减少。下面是函数图，其中突出显示了要点。 e π −0.1 0.1 0.2 0.3 x log(x) x 这意味着 log(e) e > log(π) π eπ > πe 计算器证实了这一点，eπ = 23.14 πe = 22.46。

problems.titleEn: Question 18 - Mean and variance of an AR(1) process
problems.titleZh: 问题 18 - AR(1) 过程的均值与方差
problems.promptEn: Given the AR(1) process Y_t = α_0 + α_1Y_{t-1} + ε_t, where ε_t follows Normal(0, σ_ε^2), what are E[Y_t] and Var(Y_t)?
problems.promptZh: 给定 AR(1) 过程 Y_t = α_0 + α_1Y_{t-1} + ε_t，其中 ε_t 服从 Normal(0, σ_ε^2)，求 E[Y_t] 和 Var(Y_t)。
problems.explanation: There is a long answer and a short answer. If you know the short answer, it is probably best to give it first. If your interviewer is unsatisfied, you can give the long solution. Start by assuming E(Yi) = E(Yj) and Var(Yi) = Var(Yj) (9) for all i = j. A more formal setting would require a more rigorous proof, but the interviewer might be happy to let you use this assumption for now, given the fact that their question implies it. You can also point out that, for an AR(1) process, you know it is wide-sense stationary when |α1| < 1, but the interviewer might still want proof. Using the assumption in (9), you can write E(Yi) = E(Yj) = µ, then solve for µ E(Yt) = E(α0 + α1Yt−1 + εt) E(Yt) = α0 + α1 E(Yt−1) + E(εt) µ = α0 + α1µ µ = α0 1 −α1. Similarly, for the variance, Var(Yi) = Var(Yj) = σ2 and you can solve for σ2 Var(Yt) = Var(α0 + α1Yt−1 + εt) Var(Yt) = α2 1 Var(Yt−1) + Var(εt) σ2 = α2 1σ2 + σ2 εt σ2 = σ2 εt 1 −α2. During my interview I was not aware of the short way, but the interviewer seemed content with the answer below—which is the long answer. Expand the process: Yt = α0 + α1Yt−1 + εt = α0 + α1(α0 + α1Yt−2 + εt−1) + εt = α0 + α1α0 + α2 1Yt−2 + α1εt−1 + εt = α0 + α1α0 + α2 1(α0 + α1Yt−3 + εt−2) + α1εt−1 + εt = α0 + α1α0 + α2 1α0 + α3 1Yt−3 + α2 1εt−2 + α1εt−1 + εt = α0 + α1α0 + α2 1α0 + α3 1(...) + α2 1εt−2 + α1εt−1 + εt = α0 ∞ X k=0 αk 1 + ∞ X k=0 αk 1εt−k. You only had to work to Yt−3 to notice the pattern. Taking the expectation yields E(Yt) = E α0 ∞ X k=0 αk 1 + ∞ X k=0 αk 1εt−k! = α0 ∞ X k=0 αk 1 + ∞ X k=0 αk 1 E (εt−k) = α0 ∞ X k=0 αk 1. This is a geometric series, which converges to E(Yt) = α0 1 −α1 if |α1| < 1. Likewise, for the variance you have Var(Yt) = Var α0 ∞ X k=0 αk 1 + ∞ X k=0 αk 1εt−k! = ∞ X k=0 (αk 1)2 Var (εt−k) = ∞ X k=0 (α2 1)kσ2 ε = σ2 εt 1 −α2 since the series converges if |α2 1| < 1, which will be the case if |α1| < 1. This question allows the interviewer to test your algebra, and also your familiarity with the AR(1) process, which is the simplest of the ARIMA class of models. If you mentioned time series on your CV—or if the role requires knowledge about time series—make sure you can write down the equations for a AR(p), MA(q), ARMA(p,q), and GARCH(p,q) processes. They are included in appendix C.

problems.titleEn: Question 19 - Distribution of a signed normal variable
problems.titleZh: 问题 19 - 随机变号正态变量的分布
problems.promptEn: If X follows Normal(0, 1) and Y has distribution P(Y = -1) = 1/2 and P(Y = 1) = 1/2, what is the cumulative distribution function of Z = XY?
problems.promptZh: 设 X 服从 Normal(0, 1)，且 Y 的分布为 P(Y = -1) = 1/2、P(Y = 1) = 1/2。Z = XY 的累积分布函数是什么？
problems.explanation: This is the same as question 11. Here is the proof again, with the symbols updated to match the current question. Determine the distribution of Z, FZ(x) = P(Z < x) = P(XY < x|Y = 1)P(Y = 1) + P(XY < x|Y = −1)P(Y = −1) = P(X < x)(0.5) + P(−X < x)(0.5) = (0.5)(P(X < x) + P(X ≥−x)) = (0.5)(P(X < x) + P(X < x)) symmetry = P(X < x) = FX(x) = Φ(x), which means Z ∼Normal(0, 1). Intuitively, this makes sense. You have a normal distribution multiplied by a variable that takes -1 and 1 with equal probability, so you are drawing from a normal distribution, and then randomly mirroring the value in the y-axis. Since the Normal(0, 1) distribution is symmetric about zero, this operation should not affect its distribution and thus the resulting distribution is also Normal(0, 1).

problems.titleEn: Question 20 - Zero covariance and independence
problems.titleZh: 问题 20 - 零协方差与独立性
problems.promptEn: If Cov(X, Y) = 0, are X and Y independent?
problems.promptZh: 如果 Cov(X, Y) = 0，X 和 Y 是否独立？
problems.explanation: No. The interviewer gave you the answer in the previous question (although with different symbols) and is probably checking whether you realised this. The current question and the previous question are question 11 split in two. Construct the same scenario as in question 19, but change the symbols to those of the new question. That is, let P(Z = −1) = 0.5 P(Z = 1) = 0.5 and X ∼Normal(0, 1), and Y = ZX, and then calculate Cov(X, Y ) = Cov(X, ZX) = E(XZX) −E(X) E(ZX) = E(X2Z) −E(X) E(ZX) = E(X2) E(Z) −E(X) E(Z) E(X) independence = 0 since E(Z) = 0. There is a very clear dependence between X and Y, but their covariance is zero.

problems.titleEn: Question 22 - Anagram checking in Python
problems.titleZh: 问题 22 - Python 判断异位词
problems.promptEn: Write a Python function to check whether two strings are anagrams. Do a version with sorting and a version without sorting. Why might you want a function that can do this without sorting?
problems.promptZh: 编写一个 Python 函数判断两个字符串是否为异位词。分别写出使用排序和不使用排序的版本。为什么可能需要不依赖排序的版本？
problems.explanation: The interviewer will likely explain what an anagram is before asking you this. Two words are anagrams if they have the same set of letters, like toned and noted—you can re-arrange the letters of the one to spell the other. The easiest way to check whether two strings are anagrams is to sort them and check whether they are the same. We can catch a trivial case at the beginning of the function: if the strings don't have the same length, they are definitely not anagrams. def IsAnagram(string1, string2): if len(string1) is not len(string2): return False return sorted(string1) == sorted(string2) To do this without sorting, keep track of the character counts in each word. Assume the words can only contain the lowercase characters a–z. Assign the integers 0–25 to the letters a–z, and initialise an array with 26 zeros in which to store the character counts. Then, loop over the first word and for each character encountered, increment its counter in the array by one. After this, loop over the second word, but this time, decrement the characters' counters. If the two strings are anagrams, the array will contain only zeros since the letters will have perfectly cancelled each other out. Python 2.7 actually has the function ord that converts characters into their ASCII equivalent numbers, so you can use that. def LetterToNumber(letter): return ord(letter)-ord('a') def IsAnagramNoSort(string1, string2): if len(string1) is not len(string2): return False lettercounts = [0 for i in range(26)] for letter in string1: lettercounts[LetterToNumber(letter)] += 1 for letter in string2: lettercounts[LetterToNumber(letter)] -= 1 for count in lettercounts: if count!= 0: return False return True If the strings are long, or if this is an operation you have to do often, the best version is one without sorting. Sorting is expensive: the best sorting algorithms have O(n log n) complexity. For the algorithm that doesn't sort, you only need to access each of the letters once so the complexity is O(n).

problems.explanationZh: 在问你这个问题之前，面试官可能会解释什么是字谜。如果两个单词具有相同的一组字母（例如 toned 和 noted），那么它们就是字谜词，您可以重新排列一个单词的字母来拼写另一个单词。检查两个字符串是否是字谜的最简单方法是对它们进行排序并检查它们是否相同。我们可以在函数的开头发现一个简单的情况：如果字符串的长度不同，那么它们肯定不是字谜词。 def IsAnagram(string1, string2): if len(string1) is not len(string2): return False return Sorted(string1) ==排序(string2) 要在不排序的情况下执行此操作，请跟踪每个单词中的字符计数。假设单词只能包含小写字符 a-z。将整数 0–25 分配给字母 a–z，并初始化一个包含 26 个零的数组来存储字符计数。然后，循环遍历第一个单词，对于遇到的每个字符，将其在数组中的计数器加一。之后，循环第二个单词，但这一次，减少字符的计数器。如果两个字符串是字谜词，则数组将仅包含零，因为字母将完全相互抵消。 Python 2.7 实际上具有将字符转换为其 ASCII 等效数字的函数 ord，因此您可以使用它。 def LetterToNumber(letter): return ord(letter)-ord('a') def IsAnagramNoSort(string1, string2): if len(string1) is not len(string2): return False lettercounts = [0 for i in range(26)] 对于 string1 中的字母： lettercounts[LetterToNumber(letter)] += 1 对于 string2 中的字母： lettercounts[LetterToNumber(letter)] -= 1 表示字母计数中的计数： if count!= 0: return False return True 如果字符串很长，或者如果这是您必须经常执行的操作，则最好的版本是不排序的版本。排序的成本很高：最好的排序算法的复杂度为 O(n log n)。对于不排序的算法，只需访问每个字母一次，因此复杂度为 O(n)。

problems.titleEn: Question 23 - Implement nCr
problems.titleZh: 问题 23 - 实现 nCr
problems.promptEn: Without using the standard library, write a function for nCr. Do a version with recursion and a version without recursion.
problems.promptZh: 不使用标准库，实现计算 nCr 的函数。分别写出递归版本和非递归版本。
problems.explanation: The question requires you to evaluate n k = n! (n −k)!k!, thus you need a function to calculate the factorial. The following code uses recursion to calculate it: def factorial(n): if n==0: return 1 return(n * factorial(n-1) ) def nCr(n,k): return factorial(n)/(factorial(k) * factorial(n-k)) For big numbers, this is expensive—a smarter solution exists. It is not necessary to call the factorial function three times. Also, numerical overflow is likely to occur if the nominator and denominator are both large. Consider the following example with small integers: 8 = 8! (8 −3)!3! = (8 \times 7 \times 6) 3! = 8 \times 7 \times 6 3 \times 2 \times 1. Likewise, the general case will be n k = n \times (n −1) \times... \times (n −k + 1) k \times (k −1) \times... \times 1. Since many of the integers in the nominator and denominator will cancel out, the function can exploit this, as the function below does. It also has improved numerical stability: def nCr_norecur(n,k): r = 1 for i in range(k): r *= n-i r = r // (i+1) return r You can further optimise by noting that n k = n n−k and always choosing to evaluate the one that is less work (for the function above, we prefer small values of k).

Likewise, the general case will be n k = n \times (n −1) \times... \times (n −k + 1) k \times (k −1) \times... \times 1. Since many of the integers in the nominator and denominator will cancel out, the function can exploit this, as the function below does. It also has improved numerical stability: def nCr_norecur(n,k): r = 1 for i in range(k): r *= n-i r = r // (i+1) return r You can further optimise by noting that n k = n n−k and always choosing to evaluate the one that is less work (for the function above, we prefer small values of k).
problems.explanationZh: 题目要求计算组合数 C(n, k) = n! / ((n-k)! k!)，因此最直接的做法是先写阶乘函数。递归版本可以写成：def factorial(n): if n == 0: return 1 return n * factorial(n-1)。然后 def nCr(n, k): return factorial(n) / (factorial(k) * factorial(n-k))。 不过，对于大数来说，这种方法代价较高：没有必要调用三次阶乘函数，而且当分子和分母都很大时容易出现数值溢出。以 C(8, 3) 为例：8! / ((8-3)! 3!) = (8 x 7 x 6) / (3 x 2 x 1)。一般地，C(n, k) = n x (n-1) x... x (n-k+1) / (k x (k-1) x... x 1)。分子和分母中很多整数会相互抵消，因此可以边乘边除来提高稳定性： def nCr_norecur(n, k): r = 1 for i in range(k): r *= n-i r = r // (i+1) return r 还可以利用 C(n, k) = C(n, n-k)，总是选择计算量较小的一侧；对于上面的函数，也就是优先使用较小的 k。

problems.titleEn: Question 24 - Romeo and Juliet meeting probability
problems.titleZh: 问题 24 - 罗密欧与朱丽叶相遇概率
problems.promptEn: Romeo and Juliet agree to meet between 08:00 and 09:00. Each arrives at a random time in the hour and then waits 15 minutes. What is the probability that they meet?
problems.promptZh: 罗密欧与朱丽叶约定在 08:00 到 09:00 之间见面。两人都在这一小时内随机到达，并等待 15 分钟。他们相遇的概率是多少？
problems.explanation: This is question 3.24 from Joshi et al. (2008). It is easy to overcomplicate things by thinking in great detail about what happens at the end of the interval. For instance, what if Romeo arrives at 08:58? He will wait 15 minutes, but Juliet cannot arrive after 09:00. The crux, however, is to realise that they will only meet if they arrive within 15 minutes of each other. Let the interval [0, 1] represent the hour between 08:00 and 09:00, let x be the time Romeo arrives, and y be the time Juliet arrives. They will only meet if |x −y| < 15 60 = 1 4. You have x and y independently and identically distributed Uniform(0, 1). Thus, you need to find P(|x −y| < 1/4). It is almost certain that the interviewer does not want you to solve this using integration, but rather visually on the unit square. This is a standard interview trick (described in appendix A). When x < y, you are in the top-left half of the square, and you need to consider y −x < 1/4 y < x + 1/4. When x ≥y, you are in the bottom-right half of the square, and you need to consider x −y < 1/4 y > x −1/4. The answer is the shaded portion of the square, which can be calculated quickly by breaking the square down into pieces (see below), revealing the answer as 7/16. y x y = x + 1/4 y = x −1/4 = 1 − = 1 − 3 2 = 1 −9 = 7 You can use similar logic to determine what the probability would be if they each wait t minutes or if Romeo waits twice as long as Juliet. interviews, 6 hours

problems.explanationZh: 这是 Joshi 等人提出的问题 3.24。 （2008）。详细思考间歇期结束时发生的事情很容易使事情变得过于复杂。例如，如果罗密欧 08:58 到达怎么办？他将等待 15 分钟，但朱丽叶无法在 09:00 之后到达。然而，关键是要认识到，只有在 15 分钟内到达时，他们才会见面。令间隔 [0, 1] 表示 08:00 到 09:00 之间的时间，令 x 为罗密欧到达的时间，y 为朱丽叶到达的时间。他们只有在 |x −y| 时才会相遇< 15 60 = 1 4. x 和 y 独立同分布 Uniform(0, 1)。因此，您需要找到 P(|x −y| < 1/4)。几乎可以肯定，面试官不希望你使用积分来解决这个问题，而是希望你通过单位平方来解决这个问题。这是一个标准的面试技巧（在附录 A 中描述）。当 x < y 时，您位于正方形的左上半部分，并且需要考虑 y −x < 1/4 y < x + 1/4。当 x ≥ y 时，您位于正方形的右下半部分，并且需要考虑 x −y < 1/4 y > x −1/4。答案是正方形的阴影部分，可以通过将正方形分成几块（见下文）来快速计算，得出的答案为 7/16。 y x y = x + 1/4 y = x −1/4 = 1 − = 1 − 3 2 = 1 −9 = 7 您可以使用类似的逻辑来确定如果他们每个人等待 t 分钟或者罗密欧等待时间是朱丽叶的两倍的概率。 次面试，6小时

problems.titleEn: Question 25 - Find the celebrity at a party
problems.titleZh: 问题 25 - 在聚会中找名人
problems.promptEn: Consider a party with N people. We have a function knows(a, b) that returns whether person a knows person b; it is not necessarily symmetric. Everyone at the party knows David Beckham, but he knows no one. Number the people from 1 to N. Using knows(), how would you determine Beckham's number? Now suppose Victoria Beckham is also at the party, she only knows David, David only knows her, and everyone else knows both of them and at least one other person. How would you determine their numbers?
problems.promptZh: 考虑一个有 N 个人的聚会。函数 knows(a, b) 返回 a 是否认识 b，且它不一定对称。聚会中每个人都认识 David Beckham，但他不认识任何人。将所有人编号为 1 到 N，如何用 knows() 找到 Beckham 的编号？再假设 Victoria Beckham 也在聚会中，她只认识 David，David 也只认识她，并且其他每个人都认识他们二人且至少还认识一个其他人。如何确定他们的编号？
problems.answer: For one celebrity, use the standard candidate-elimination scan, then verify the candidate. For the David/Victoria variant, identify the two people whose only outgoing edge is to each other and verify that every other person knows both of them.
problems.answerEn: For one celebrity, use the standard candidate-elimination scan, then verify the candidate. For the David/Victoria variant, identify the two people whose only outgoing edge is to each other and verify that every other person knows both of them.
problems.answerZh: 单个名人情形用标准候选人淘汰扫描，然后验证候选人。David/Victoria 变体中，找出两个只互相认识的人，并验证其他所有人都认识他们二人。
problems.explanation:
Model the party as a directed graph: knows(a,b) is an edge from a to b. In the first version, David Beckham is a celebrity vertex with out-degree 0 and in-degree N-1.

A linear candidate scan finds the only possible celebrity. Start with candidate c=1. For each i=2,...,N, ask knows(c,i). If c knows i, then c cannot be Beckham, so set c=i. If c does not know i, then i cannot be Beckham, because everyone knows Beckham. After this scan, verify c by checking that c knows nobody and everybody else knows c. The scan uses N-1 calls and the verification uses O(N) more calls.

In the David/Victoria version, the two special people each know exactly one person, namely each other; every other person knows both of them and at least one additional person. One direct method is to find the two rows of the adjacency matrix with exactly one outgoing 1 and check that these two rows point to each other. Then verify that every other person knows both candidates. This determines the pair, although the data may not distinguish which one is David and which one is Victoria unless the labels themselves are observable.

If c does not know i, then i cannot be Beckham, because everyone knows Beckham. After this scan, verify c by checking that c knows nobody and everybody else knows c. The scan uses N-1 calls and the verification uses O(N) more calls.

In the David/Victoria version, the two special people each know exactly one person, namely each other; every other person knows both of them and at least one additional person. problems.explanationZh:
把聚会看成有向图：knows(a,b) 表示从 a 指向 b 的边。在第一种情形中，David Beckham 是一个出度为 0、入度为 N-1 的“名人”顶点。

可以用线性候选人扫描找到唯一可能的名人。令候选人为 c=1。对 i=2,...,N 依次询问 knows(c,i)。如果 c 认识 i，那么 c 不可能是 Beckham，于是令 c=i；如果 c 不认识 i，那么 i 不可能是 Beckham，因为所有人都认识 Beckham。扫描结束后，再验证 c：c 不认识任何人，并且其他所有人都认识 c。扫描需要 N-1 次调用，验证还需要 O(N) 次调用。

在 David/Victoria 版本中，这两个特殊人物各自只认识一个人，也就是彼此；其他每个人都认识他们二人，并且至少还认识一个其他人。一个直接方法是找出邻接矩阵中恰好只有一个出边为 1 的两行，并检查这两行是否互相指向对方；随后验证其他所有人都认识这两个候选人。这样可以确定这对人，但如果仅靠 knows() 关系本身，通常无法区分谁是 David、谁是 Victoria。

problems.titleEn: Question 26 - Find a duplicate without sorting
problems.titleZh: 问题 26 - 不排序找重复数
problems.promptEn: You have an unsorted array of N integers [n1, n2, ..., nN]. All integers are unique except two entries that are equal. These are arbitrary integers, not necessarily 1 to N. How would you find the duplicate? Give an answer that does not rely on sorting, then give an answer with sorting and discuss your favorite sorting algorithm.
problems.promptZh: 有一个包含 N 个整数的未排序数组 [n1, n2, ..., nN]。除两个相同的元素外，其余整数都唯一；这些整数是任意整数，不一定是 1 到 N。如何找出重复元素？给出一个不依赖排序的方法，再给出一个使用排序的方法，并讨论你喜欢的排序算法。
problems.answer: Without sorting, scan once with a hash set and return the first value already seen. With sorting, sort the array, then scan adjacent entries for equality. A good favorite sorting algorithm to discuss is mergesort: O(N log N) time, stable, divide-and-conquer, and easy to reason about.
problems.answerEn: Without sorting, scan once with a hash set and return the first value already seen. With sorting, sort the array, then scan adjacent entries for equality. A good favorite sorting algorithm to discuss is mergesort: O(N log N) time, stable, divide-and-conquer, and easy to reason about.
problems.answerZh: 不排序时，用哈希集合扫描一遍，遇到已经出现过的值就返回。排序时，先排序再检查相邻元素是否相等。适合讨论的排序算法是归并排序：时间复杂度 O(N log N)，稳定，采用分治思想，也容易解释。

problems.titleEn: Question 27 - One bullet and 100 prisoners
problems.titleZh: 问题 27 - 一颗子弹与 100 名囚犯
problems.promptEn: You are guarding 100 prisoners in a field, and you have a gun with a single bullet. If any one prisoner has a non-zero probability of surviving, he will attempt to escape. If a prisoner is certain of death, he will not attempt escape. How do you stop them from escaping?
problems.promptZh: 你在一片空地看守 100 名囚犯，手中有一把只有一颗子弹的枪。如果任意一名囚犯有非零生存概率，他就会试图逃跑；如果他确定会死，就不会逃跑。你如何阻止他们逃跑？
problems.explanation: The solution to this question requires many assumptions on the rationality of the criminals which probably don't hold in practice. You should also ask some further questions to establish that you are allowed to address the criminals before they make a run for it, which probably means you have a very loud voice or a megaphone. Good interviewers will tell you this as part of the question, while others might want you to prod. Start with n = 1. Were there a single murderer, you would shoot him if he tried to escape. Because he would know the probability of survival is zero, he wouldn't try. Were there only two murderers in the field, each would have a probability of surviving of 0.5, and thus would attempt to escape. But, if you told one of the two that you would definitely shoot him if the two of them attempted to escape, he wouldn't make an attempt, bringing you back to the situation with a single murderer. You can generalise this outcome to three or more murderers, but for any given group you need to identify one murderer as the so-called “sacrificial escapee”, and make him aware of it. The easiest way is to number them from 1 to n, and tell them that, for any group that attempts an escape, the member with the largest number will be shot. By induction, no group will attempt an escape. If you don't have time to number them, the go-to solution is to tell the murderers that, in the event of an attempted escape, the tallest member of the group will be shot. Though this is an illogical question, try not to get frustrated and point this out to the interviewer. It is asked in the same spirit as questions such as “how many ping pong balls can fit into an Airbus A380,” or “how many ties are sold in London in a year.” The interviewer wants to see your thought process. Through all of my interviews I only got a question like this once and while I think they are rare in quantitative interviews, I believe they are more prevalent in investment banking interviews in general. I would suggest you look up two or three of these, and study the format of the solutions.

problems.explanationZh: 要解决这个问题，需要对犯罪分子的理性做出许多假设，而这些假设在实践中可能并不成立。您还应该问一些进一步的问题，以确定您可以在犯罪分子逃跑之前与他们交谈，这可能意味着您的声音很大或有扩音器。好的面试官会在问题中告诉你这一点，而其他人可能希望你督促。从 n = 1 开始。如果有一个凶手，如果他试图逃跑，你就会开枪射杀他。因为他知道生存的可能性为零，所以他不会尝试。如果现场只有两名凶手，则每人的生存概率为 0.5，因此会尝试逃跑。但是，如果你告诉两人中的一个，如果他们两个试图逃跑，你肯定会开枪射杀他，他就不会尝试，让你回到只有一个凶手的情况。你可以将这个结果推广到三名或更多凶手，但对于任何特定群体，你需要将一名凶手确定为所谓的“牺牲逃亡者”，并让他意识到这一点。最简单的方法就是将他们从 1 到 n 编号，并告诉他们，对于任何试图逃跑的团体，编号最大的成员将被枪杀。根据归纳法，没有任何团体会尝试逃跑。如果你没有时间给他们编号，首选的解决方案是告诉凶手，如果他们试图逃跑，那么该组织中最高的成员将被枪杀。尽管这是一个不合逻辑的问题，但不要感到沮丧，并向面试官指出这一点。提出这个问题的精神与诸如“空客 A380 可以容纳多少个乒乓球”或“伦敦一年销售多少条领带”等问题的精神相同。面试官想了解你的思维过程。在我所有的面试中，我只遇到过一次这样的问题，虽然我认为它们在定量面试中很少见，但我相信它们在一般投资银行面试中更为普遍。我建议你查找其中的两到三个，并研究解决方案的格式。

problems.titleEn: Question 28 - Significance tests in logistic regression
problems.titleZh: 问题 28 - 逻辑回归参数显著性检验
problems.promptEn: What significance tests are used for parameters estimated in a logistic regression? How are they different from those used for a linear regression?

problems.titleEn: Question 29 - Measuring two lengths with minimum variance
problems.titleZh: 问题 29 - 用最小方差测量两段长度
problems.promptEn: You have two pieces of wood of lengths a and b, where a < b, and a measuring apparatus with measurement-error variance σ^2. It costs £1 per use and you only have £2. What is the best strategy to measure a and b with minimal variance?
problems.promptZh: 你有两段长度为 a 和 b 的木头，其中 a < b；测量工具的测量误差方差为 σ^2。每次使用花费 1 英镑，你只有 2 英镑。以最小方差测量 a 和 b 的最佳策略是什么？
problems.explanation: If you measure a and b individually, you get E(a) = a Var(a) = σ2 E(b) = b Var(b) = σ2 Using £2, you've measured each one with an error of σ2, but you can do better by measuring the sum of the two sticks, s = a + b, and the difference between the two d = a −b: a b b −a a b a + b Solve for a and b in terms of s and d, and see what the mean and variance of the estimates are. a = s + d E(a) = E s + d = a Var(a) = Var s + d = 1 4(Var(s) + Var(d)) = 1 2σ2 Likewise, for b b = s −d E(b) = E s −d = b Var(b) = Var s −d = 1 2σ2 Using £2 and the new strategy, you've measured each stick with an error of σ2/2. What happened here? As if by magic, the variance has been reduced by half. The trick is that you used the tools to get two measurements of a and b each. Each additional measurement will reduce the variance of the estimator.
problems.explanationEn: If you measure a and b individually, you get E(a) = a Var(a) = σ2 E(b) = b Var(b) = σ2 Using £2, you've measured each one with an error of σ2, but you can do better by measuring the sum of the two sticks, s = a + b, and the difference between the two d = a −b: a b b −a a b a + b Solve for a and b in terms of s and d, and see what the mean and variance of the estimates are. a = s + d E(a) = E s + d = a Var(a) = Var s + d = 1 4(Var(s) + Var(d)) = 1 2σ2 Likewise, for b b = s −d E(b) = E s −d = b Var(b) = Var s −d = 1 2σ2 Using £2 and the new strategy, you've measured each stick with an error of σ2/2. What happened here? As if by magic, the variance has been reduced by half. The trick is that you used the tools to get two measurements of a and b each. Each additional measurement will reduce the variance of the estimator.
problems.explanationZh: 如果单独测量 a 和 b，则得到 E(a) = a Var(a) = σ2 E(b) = b Var(b) = σ2 使用 £2，您测量的每一个都具有 σ2 的误差，但是您可以通过测量两根棍子的总和 s = a + b 以及两者之间的差 d = a −b：a b b −a a b a + b 来做得更好。 d，看看估计值的均值和方差是多少。 a = s + d E(a) = E s + d = a Var(a) = Var s + d = 1 4(Var(s) + Var(d)) = 1 2σ2 同样，对于 b b = s −d E(b) = E s −d = b Var(b) = Var s −d = 1 2σ2 使用 £2 和新策略，您测量的每根棍子的误差为σ2/2。这里发生了什么？就像变魔术一样，方差减少了一半。诀窍在于您使用这些工具分别获得 a 和 b 的两个测量值。每个额外的测量都会减少估计量的方差。

problems.titleEn: Question 30 - Minimum number of socks
problems.titleZh: 问题 30 - 袜子数量的最小可能值
problems.promptEn: A bag contains N socks, some black and some red. If two random socks are picked, the probability that both are red is 1/2. What is the smallest possible value of N?
problems.promptZh: 一个袋子中有 N 只袜子，其中一些是黑色，一些是红色。随机抽取两只袜子，它们都是红色的概率为 1/2。N 的最小可能值是多少？
problems.answer: The smallest possible value is N=4, with r=3 red socks.
problems.answerEn: The smallest possible value is N=4, with r=3 red socks.
problems.answerZh: 最小可能值为 N=4，此时红袜子数 r=3。
problems.explanation:
Let r be the number of red socks among N socks. The probability that two socks drawn without replacement are both red is

P(both red) = (r/N) * ((r-1)/(N-1)) = 1/2.

For N=2, both socks would have to be red, giving probability 1 rather than 1/2. For N=3, no integer r satisfies r(r-1)/(3*2)=1/2. For N=4, choosing r=3 gives (3/4)*(2/3)=1/2.

Therefore the smallest possible value of N is 4.
problems.explanationEn:
Let r be the number of red socks among N socks. The probability that two socks drawn without replacement are both red is

P(both red) = (r/N) * ((r-1)/(N-1)) = 1/2.

For N=2, both socks would have to be red, giving probability 1 rather than 1/2. For N=3, no integer r satisfies r(r-1)/(3*2)=1/2. For N=4, choosing r=3 gives (3/4)*(2/3)=1/2.

Therefore the smallest possible value of N is 4.
problems.explanationZh:
设 N 只袜子中有 r 只是红色。不放回抽取两只袜子都为红色的概率为

P(两只都是红袜子) = (r/N) * ((r-1)/(N-1)) = 1/2。

当 N=2 时，若两只都是红色则概率为 1，不符合题意。当 N=3 时，没有整数 r 使 r(r-1)/(3*2)=1/2。当 N=4 时，取 r=3，有 (3/4)*(2/3)=1/2。

因此 N 的最小可能值为 4。

problems.titleEn: Question 32 - Examples of non-linear models
problems.titleZh: 问题 32 - 非线性模型示例
problems.promptEn: What are some non-linear models?
problems.promptZh: 有哪些非线性模型？
problems.explanation: Some examples of non-linear models are: • Survival models, which usually study a non-linear hazard rate h(t) • Generalised Linear Models (GLM), which can have non-linear link functions (the logistic regression uses the logit link function, and the Gamma GLM uses the log link function) • Gaussian processes (and other non-parametric regressions) • Neural networks, which are an approximation of Gaussian processes, as shown by Murphy (2012, chap. 15.4) using the proof that was first pointed out by Neal (1996): as the number of hidden layers in a neural net goes to infinity, it becomes a Gaussian process In fact, a broad class of non-linear models can be presented in the following way: y ∼MultivariateNormal(g(X, θ), Σ) where g(X, θ) is some non-linear function of the parameters and the inputs. You can make this more general by replacing the multivariate normal distribution, y ∼Dist(g(X, θ)) where y is assumed to follow some distribution and g(X, θ) is some function that links the inputs to the parameters of the distribution. See Murphy (2012) for an exhaustive treatment of this idea.
problems.explanationEn: Some examples of non-linear models are: • Survival models, which usually study a non-linear hazard rate h(t) • Generalised Linear Models (GLM), which can have non-linear link functions (the logistic regression uses the logit link function, and the Gamma GLM uses the log link function) • Gaussian processes (and other non-parametric regressions) • Neural networks, which are an approximation of Gaussian processes, as shown by Murphy (2012, chap. 15.4) using the proof that was first pointed out by Neal (1996): as the number of hidden layers in a neural net goes to infinity, it becomes a Gaussian process In fact, a broad class of non-linear models can be presented in the following way: y ∼MultivariateNormal(g(X, θ), Σ) where g(X, θ) is some non-linear function of the parameters and the inputs. You can make this more general by replacing the multivariate normal distribution, y ∼Dist(g(X, θ)) where y is assumed to follow some distribution and g(X, θ) is some function that links the inputs to the parameters of the distribution. See Murphy (2012) for an exhaustive treatment of this idea.
problems.explanationZh: 非线性模型的一些示例包括： • 生存模型，通常研究非线性风险率 h(t) • 广义线性模型 (GLM)，可以具有非线性链接函数（逻辑回归使用 logit 链接函数，Gamma GLM 使用对数链接函数） • 高斯过程（和其他非参数回归） • 神经网络，它是高斯过程的近似，如 Murphy 所示（2012 年，第 15.4 章）使用 Neal (1996) 首次指出的证明：随着神经网络中隐藏层的数量趋向无穷大，它变成高斯过程。事实上，一类广泛的非线性模型可以通过以下方式表示： y ∼MultivariateNormal(g(X, θ), Σ)，其中 g(X, θ) 是参数和输入的一些非线性函数。您可以通过替换多元正态分布 y ∼Dist(g(X, θ)) 来使其更加通用，其中假设 y 遵循某种分布，而 g(X, θ) 是将输入链接到分布参数的函数。请参阅 Murphy (2012) 对这一想法的详尽阐述。答案 33.a)：（第 48 页上的问题） SELECT Employee_name, a.Department, Salary, avg_dept_sal from salaries 作为 INNER JOIN ( SELECT Department, avg(Salary) as avg_dept_sal from salaries GROUP BY Department ) as b where a.Department = b.Department 我包含用于创建表并执行此查询的代码附录 B.3。答案 33.b)：（第 48 页的问题）有很多方法可以做到这一点，包括合并、排序和连接。我发现最简单、最清晰的方法是使用 LIMIT 和 OFFSET，它们存在于所有 SQL 实现中。逻辑是按薪水降序排序，然后将行偏移一位，并仅返回一个条目。结果将是第二高的条目。您可以轻松更改偏移量以返回第 n 个最高工资： SELECT Employee_name, Department, Salary FROM salaries ORDER BY Salary DESC LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1 附录 B.3 包含加载表和测试此查询的代码。

problems.titleEn: Question 33 - SQL average department salary
problems.titleZh: 问题 33 - SQL 计算部门平均薪资
problems.promptEn: Write some SQL queries. Consider a table with the columns Employee name, Department, and Salary. Add a column showing the average department salary for each employee.
problems.promptZh: 编写 SQL 查询。考虑一个包含 Employee name、Department 和 Salary 三列的表。请新增一列，显示每位员工所在部门的平均薪资。
problems.explanation: If the SQL dialect supports window functions, use: SELECT Employee_name, Department, Salary, AVG(Salary) OVER (PARTITION BY Department) AS avg_department_salary FROM salaries; Without window functions, join each row to a grouped subquery: SELECT a.Employee_name, a.Department, a.Salary, b.avg_department_salary FROM salaries AS a JOIN (SELECT Department, AVG(Salary) AS avg_department_salary FROM salaries GROUP BY Department) AS b ON a.Department = b.Department.
problems.explanationEn: If the SQL dialect supports window functions, use: SELECT Employee_name, Department, Salary, AVG(Salary) OVER (PARTITION BY Department) AS avg_department_salary FROM salaries; Without window functions, join each row to a grouped subquery: SELECT a.Employee_name, a.Department, a.Salary, b.avg_department_salary FROM salaries AS a JOIN (SELECT Department, AVG(Salary) AS avg_department_salary FROM salaries GROUP BY Department) AS b ON a.Department = b.Department.
problems.explanationZh: 如果 SQL 方言支持窗口函数，可以写：SELECT Employee_name, Department, Salary, AVG(Salary) OVER (PARTITION BY Department) AS avg_department_salary FROM salaries; 如果不支持窗口函数，则先按部门聚合，再与原表连接：SELECT a.Employee_name, a.Department, a.Salary, b.avg_department_salary FROM salaries AS a JOIN (SELECT Department, AVG(Salary) AS avg_department_salary FROM salaries GROUP BY Department) AS b ON a.Department = b.Department。

problems.titleEn: Question 34 - SQL customers with changed details
problems.titleZh: 问题 34 - SQL 查询信息发生变化的客户
problems.promptEn: You are given a table with columns Customer ID, Name, datestart, and dateend. Write a query to return all customers who have made changes to their details.
problems.promptZh: 给定一个包含 Customer ID、Name、datestart 和 dateend 四列的表。编写查询，返回所有曾更改过个人信息的客户。
problems.explanation: Look at the table and make an assumption on which columns will give this information. Find the entries in the Customer ID column that appear multiple times. When a customer changes their details, they keep the same ID. When you want to test conditions on calculated columns, use HAVING instead of WHERE: SELECT CustomerID FROM customers GROUP BY CustomerID HAVING count(CustomerID) > 1 Appendix B.3 contains code to load the table and test this query. SQL questions aren't that common. During my interview, the interviewer asked me whether I was familiar with SQL before asking these questions (I listed it on my CV). Had I said “no”, they probably would have asked me data-cleaning questions in other languages. Practising SQL is tedious due to the amount of work required to get it working on your system. Some installations—like OracleDB or Microsoft SQL server—are prohibitively expensive for someone who just wants to practise. Postgresql and MySQL are free and open source, but they require multiple libraries to be installed and access management to be configured (even if you just want to run queries locally). It might be worth the effort, though, to get an understanding of the architecture. The path of least resistance is certainly sqlite, used with Python. Most Python installations come with sqlite as standard, so it doesn't require any other libraries. I suggest using the code included in B.3 along with a dataset you are familiar with to do some cleaning and munging using queries (as opposed to using R or Pandas data frames), especially if you list SQL on your CV, but haven't used it recently. A working knowledge of MySQL is the logical final step.

problems.titleEn: Question 36 - R data structures
problems.titleZh: 问题 36 - R 的数据结构
problems.promptEn: Answer the following question about R: what are some data structures used in R?
problems.promptZh: 回答这个关于 R 的问题：R 中常用的数据结构有哪些？
problems.explanation: Common R data structures include vectors, lists, matrices, arrays, factors, data frames, and data.table/tibble-style tabular objects. Vectors are homogeneous one-dimensional containers; lists can hold mixed types and nested objects; matrices and arrays are multi-dimensional homogeneous containers; factors encode categorical variables; data frames store table-like data with columns of possibly different types.

problems.explanationZh: R 中常见的数据结构包括向量、列表、矩阵、数组、因子、数据框，以及 data.table/tibble 这类表格对象。向量是一维且元素类型一致的容器；列表可以包含不同类型和嵌套对象；矩阵和数组是多维且类型一致的容器；因子用于表示分类变量；数据框用于存储表格数据，各列可以有不同类型。
